title: ESDC Wellness Gym: SharePoint Redesign

star.situation: The Workplace Mental Health team's resource hub lived on ESDC's legacy intranet, where they couldn't update content without IT and employees struggled to find anything.
star.task: Move it to SharePoint so the team maintains it directly and 40,000 employees can find resources without getting lost.
star.action: I ran four moderated interviews to understand how employees reach for mental-health resources, built personas, then tested the proposed information architecture with two rounds of tree testing (29 participants). Three resources scored below 30%. I pulled the career page out of the wellness IA, surfaced the CIRC crisis resource into a homepage Quick Links rail, and relabeled the Diversity Network.
star.result: The revised architecture shipped as a working SharePoint site the team owns and updates without IT tickets, with an FAQ for employees new to SharePoint.

_internalReferences: WMH product owner / ESDC supervisor [confirm name + permission]

star.situation: A federal mental-health team had access to AI tools but no clear, safe way to adopt them.
star.task: Test the tools and help the team use them well.
star.action: I tested agentic AI and RAG systems, designed personalized AI workflows for the team, and aligned the approach with the psychological factors behind healthy workplace adoption. I wrote training resources, reference guides, and process documentation so colleagues could use the tools without me in the room.
star.result: The team gained vetted AI workflows and the documentation to use organizational AI tools on their own.

title: City of Calgary: Digital Equity Pilot

star.situation: Calgary funded a pilot to test 7 essential digital services with residents most affected by the digital divide: newcomers, older adults, and people with disabilities who use assistive tech. People with disabilities were hardest to recruit, since our criteria required them to test independently on a desktop.
star.task: Recruit and run usability testing across four groups of eight, on timeline and budget.
star.action: I built a referral program with monetary incentives, sent personalized outreach for snowball recruitment, and added pre-interviews to walk candidates through what to expect. Those calls surfaced that several screen-reader users preferred their phones, so I documented it and pushed to relax the desktop-only criterion.
star.result: We mapped 18 equity challenges across all 7 services, the City proposed a follow-up round adding mobile testing, and one tester became an advocate who referred others. The results are still discussed across the City's digital teams.

star.situation: Calgary Economic Development's WIL program built a website and employer portal to connect students with placements, with part of the portal run by a third-party vendor.
star.task: Evaluate usability and inclusiveness across two phases and recommend prioritized fixes.
star.action: I ran moderated remote usability tests with scripts adapted per user group, plus heuristic analysis and accessibility testing with a screen-reader user. After two projects where vendor constraints surfaced late, I rebuilt the client-intake process to ask up front about third-party tools and limitations.
star.result: Delivered usability and heuristic reports across both phases on time, prioritized fixes with an impact-effort matrix tied to client constraints, and improved intake so future projects scoped vendor limits correctly.

star.situation: IncluCity ran inclusive research for municipal, nonprofit, and social-impact clients, with researchers of varying experience and no shared operating system.
star.task: Build the operations layer that lets a volunteer-heavy research org deliver consistent, high-quality work at scale.
star.action: I built a research-operations framework for 50+ researchers covering onboarding, training, contract administration, data management, and QA. I supported proposals and client acquisition from first contact through delivery, and ran inclusive-design workshops and panels.
star.result: The framework standardized how projects ran across the org, I delivered 140+ stakeholder interviews into client-ready reports, and inclusive-design events reached 100+ attendees.

star.situation: Beneva, one of Canada's largest mutual insurers, wanted to know where its online quote flow lost users.
star.task: As part of a four-person team, evaluate the home and auto quote experiences and rank what to fix first.
star.action: We ran moderated usability tests with surveys, scored the experience with SUS, and rated each finding by severity. The clearest failure showed up when participants edited a home quote: the price card still read $0.00 because the Recalculate Price button sat above where people had stopped looking. Six of twelve missed it and concluded the system was broken.
star.result: We delivered a SUS baseline of 79, eight prioritized fixes with severity ratings and an impact-by-effort matrix, and recommended dynamic pricing as the lead change so price updates without a button.

title: Tech3Lab: GenAI Tutoring Study (Thesis + FLAIRS)
methods: Multimodal physiological measurement (eye-tracking, pupillometry, EEG, ECG)

star.situation: AI tutors personalize learning. We had little direct evidence of how that personalization affects a learner's cognitive load in real time.
star.task: Design and run a study measuring how Generative AI personalization changes behaviour and cognitive load, rigorous enough for both a thesis and a publication.
star.action: I designed the full study from literature review and ethics through data collection, then captured multimodal physiological signals (eye-tracking, pupillometry, ECG, EEG) while participants worked through AI-mediated tasks. I analyzed the combined behavioural and physiological data to read cognitive load instead of relying on self-report.
star.result: The work became my master's thesis (honours with great distinction, 3.85 GPA) and a conference-track FLAIRS publication, with design recommendations for AI tutor systems. I was lead author on the related paper on LLM-based personalized math tutoring.

_internalReferences: A. Karran (supervisor); P-M. Leger (director) [confirm + ask permission]

title: UQAM SEXODEV: Workflow Automation
job: UQAM SEXODEV (Workflow Automation & Process Optimization Consultant)
methods: AI workflow design (recordings to tasks / summaries / docs)

star.situation: A research chair's small remote team was buried in manual coordination. Notes were scattered across OneDrive and SharePoint, follow-ups depended on whoever took notes, and the Chair carried too much operational load.
star.task: Diagnose the bottlenecks and build a system that cuts that load and improves accountability.
star.action: I started with stakeholder interviews to find where work actually stalled, then designed AI-driven workflows that turn meeting recordings into assigned tasks, individual action summaries, and filed documentation. I set up a Notion workspace as the single source of truth with naming conventions, folder structure, and permissions, and wrote onboarding guides so the process survives staff turnover.
star.result: The team replaced manual note-taking with an automated pipeline and consolidated scattered files into one organized workspace.

title: Allen & Thomas: E-Discovery AI
job: Allen & Thomas, LLP (AI Search & Implementation Specialist)

star.situation: A law firm running an investigation needed to search and review a large document database across a remote team, and the existing approach was slow.
star.task: Improve how the database was searched and prioritize what got reviewed first.
star.action: I implemented e-discovery systems and tooling, developed search strategies and data-mining processes, and applied supervised learning to rank documents for review. I coordinated the review across the team and built repeatable remote workflows.
star.result: The team searched and reviewed the database more efficiently, with a prioritized review queue in place of a flat pile.

title: Notion to AI to Anki Pipeline

star.situation: My Johns Hopkins coursework lived in Notion as text, tables, and lecture screenshots. Reading it back didn't make it stick, and hand-writing Anki cards was slow enough that I never kept up.
star.task: Point a tool at a Notion page and get reviewed spaced-repetition cards out the other end, while staying in control of what I study.
star.action: I wrote the architecture as a spec first, then directed Claude Code to build a two-stage pipeline. Stage one reads the full page and screenshots and sends them to a vision model that returns atomic question-and-answer cards, which land in a review file I prune. Stage two loads the approved cards into Anki through AnkiConnect.
star.result: The first run produced 58 accurate cards from 1,876 content blocks and 137 screenshots for about two cents, and the model derived 25 hierarchical week tags on its own from my lecture headings. None needed deleting. The manual step that kept me off spaced repetition is gone, and the review checkpoint stayed in by design.

star.situation: UX Montreal is a local design community that needs consistent programming and accessible events.
star.task: Co-direct the organization with Farah and Scarlett, running events and building the community.
star.action: Programmed events and used sliding-scale ticketing through Luma so cost wasn't a barrier to attending.
